title: cenchrus sphacelatus
intro: Cenchrus sphacelatus, also known as the sandbur or in some regions, the sand grass, is a type of annual grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is commonly used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The health benefits of Cenchrus sphacelatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antiseptic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared into herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
